RemoteRegistry->>StaticRegistry: this.data = manifest\n                RemoteRegistry-->>Client: load resolved (isLoaded=true)\n            else invalid manifest\n                RemoteRegistry-->>Client: throw EngineError (i18nKey: registry.invalidFormat)\n            end\n        else sri mismatch\n            RemoteRegistry-->>Client: throw EngineError (i18nKey: registry.sriMismatch)\n        end\n    else fetch failed or timeout\n        RemoteRegistry-->>Client: throw EngineError (i18nKey: registry.fetchFailed / registry.timeout)\n    end\n It allows for dynamic fetching of engine manifests from remote sources and provides flexible ways to combine results from multiple engines. The changes also improve code quality and security through stricter type checking and robust Subresource Integrity validation.\n\n### Highlights\n\n* **Engine Registry Infrastructure**: Implemented `RemoteRegistry` for fetching engine manifests from external URLs and established a LIFO-based resolution chain in `EngineBridge` for registry prioritization.\n* **Advanced Ensemble Strategies**: ...(config.sources || {}),\n  };\n\n  if (!sources.main) {\n    // TODO: Use i18n for this error message\n    throw new Error('Engine \"edax\" requires a \"main\" source, but it was not found in the };\n```","created_at":"2026-02-24T09:51:55Z","updated_at":"2026-02-24T09:51:55Z","html_url":"https://github.com/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pull/42#discussion_r2845715584","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pulls/42","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pulls/comments/2845715584"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pull/42#discussion_r2845715584"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pulls/42"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pulls/comments/2845715584/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":37,"original_start_line":26,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":40,"original_line":32,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":38,"position":46,"subject_type":"line"},{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/hdkz-dev/multi-game-engines/pulls/comments/2845715590","pull_request_review_id":3846424597,"id":2845715590,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDORJ0gd86pniyG","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,125 @@\n+---\n+name: code-review\n+description: \"AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Default code-review skill. Trigger for any explicit review request AND autonomously when the agent thinks a review is needed (code/PR/quality/security).\"\n+---\n+\n+# CodeRabbit Code Review\n+\n+AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles without manual intervention.\n+\n+## Capabilities\n+\n+- Finds bugs, security issues, and quality risks in changed code\n+- Groups findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Info)\n+- Works on staged, committed, or all changes; supports base branch/commit\n+- Provides fix suggestions (`--plain`) or minimal output for agents (`--prompt-only`)\n+\n+## When to Use\n+\n+When user asks to:\n+\n+- Review code changes / Review my code\n+- Check code quality / Find bugs or security issues\n+- Get PR feedback / Pull request review\n+- What's wrong with my code / my changes\n+- Run coderabbit / Use coderabbit\n+\n+## How to Review\n+\n+### 1. Check Prerequisites\n+\n+```bash\n+coderabbit --version 2>/dev/null || echo \"NOT_INSTALLED\"\n+coderabbit auth status 2>&1\n+```\n+\n+**If CLI not installed**, tell user:\n+\n+```text\n+Please install CodeRabbit CLI first:\n+curl -fsSL https://cli.coderabbit.ai/install.sh | sh\n+```\n+\n+**If not authenticated**, tell user:\n+\n+```text\n+Please authenticate first:\n+coderabbit auth login\n+```\n+\n+### 2. Run Review\n+\n+Use `--prompt-only` for minimal output optimized for AI agents:\n+\n+```bash\n+coderabbit review --prompt-only\n+```\n+\n+Or use `--plain` for detailed feedback with fix suggestions:\n+\n+```bash\n+coderabbit review --plain\n+```\n+\n+**Options:**\n+\n+| Flag             | Description                            |\n+| ---------------- | -------------------------------------- |\n+| `-t all`         | All changes (default)                  |\n+| `-t committed`   | Committed changes only                 |\n+| `-t uncommitted` | Uncommitted changes only               |\n+| `--base main`    | Compare against specific branch        |\n+| `--base-commit`  | Compare against specific commit hash   |\n+| `--prompt-only`  | Minimal output optimized for AI agents |\n+| `--plain`        | Detailed feedback with fix suggestions |\n+\n+**Shorthand:** `cr` is an alias for `coderabbit`:\n+\n+```bash\n+cr review --prompt-only\n+```\n+\n+### 3. Present Results\n+\n+Group findings by severity:\n+\n+1. **Critical** - Security vulnerabilities, data loss risks, crashes\n+2. **Warning** - Bugs, performance issues, anti-patterns\n+3. **Info** - Style issues, suggestions, minor improvements\n+\n+Create a task list for issues found that need to be addressed.\n+\n+### 4. Fix Issues (Autonomous Workflow)\n+\n+When user requests implementation + review:\n+\n+1. Implement the requested feature\n+2. Run `coderabbit review --prompt-only`\n+3. Create task list from findings\n+4. Fix critical and warning issues systematically\n+5. Re-run review to verify fixes\n+6. Default code-review skill. Trigger for any explicit review request AND autonomously when the agent thinks a review is needed (code/PR/quality/security).\"\n+---\n+\n+# CodeRabbit Code Review\n+\n+AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles without manual intervention.\n+\n+## Capabilities\n+\n+- Finds bugs, security issues, and quality risks in changed code\n+- Groups findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Info)\n+- Works on staged, committed, or all changes; supports base branch/commit\n+- Provides fix suggestions (`--plain`) or minimal output for agents (`--prompt-only`)\n+\n+## When to Use\n+\n+When user asks to:\n+\n+- Review code changes / Review my code\n+- Check code quality / Find bugs or security issues\n+- Get PR feedback / Pull request review\n+- What's wrong with my code / my changes\n+- Run coderabbit / Use coderabbit\n+\n+## How to Review\n+\n+### 1. Check Prerequisites\n+\n+```bash\n+coderabbit --version 2>/dev/null || echo \"NOT_INSTALLED\"\n+coderabbit auth status 2>&1\n+```\n+\n+**If CLI not installed**, tell user:\n+\n+```text\n+Please install CodeRabbit CLI first:\n+curl -fsSL https://cli.coderabbit.ai/install.sh | sh\n+```\n+\n+**If not authenticated**, tell user:\n+\n+```text\n+Please authenticate first:\n+coderabbit auth login\n+```\n+\n+### 2. Run Review\n+\n+Use `--prompt-only` for minimal output optimized for AI agents:\n+\n+```bash\n+coderabbit review --prompt-only\n+```\n+\n+Or use `--plain` for detailed feedback with fix suggestions:\n+\n+```bash\n+coderabbit review --plain\n+```\n+\n+**Options:**\n+\n+| Flag             | Description                            |\n+| ---------------- | -------------------------------------- |\n+| `-t all`         | All changes (default)                  |\n+| `-t committed`   | Committed changes only                 |\n+| `-t uncommitted` | Uncommitted changes only               |\n+| `--base main`    | Compare against specific branch        |\n+| `--base-commit`  | Compare against specific commit hash   |\n+| `--prompt-only`  | Minimal output optimized for AI agents |\n+| `--plain`        | Detailed feedback with fix suggestions |\n+\n+**Shorthand:** `cr` is an alias for `coderabbit`:\n+\n+```bash\n+cr review --prompt-only\n+```\n+\n+### 3. Present Results\n+\n+Group findings by severity:\n+\n+1. **Critical** - Security vulnerabilities, data loss risks, crashes\n+2. **Warning** - Bugs, performance issues, anti-patterns\n+3. **Info** - Style issues, suggestions, minor improvements\n+\n+Create a task list for issues found that need to be addressed.\n+\n+### 4. Fix Issues (Autonomous Workflow)\n+\n+When user requests implementation + review:\n+\n+1. Implement the requested feature\n+2. Run `coderabbit review --prompt-only`\n+3. Create task list from findings\n+4. Fix critical and warning issues systematically\n+5. Re-run review to verify fixes\n+6. Repeat until clean or only info-level issues remain\n+\n+### 5. Review Specific Changes\n+\n+**Review only uncommitted changes:**\n+\n+```bash\n+cr review --prompt-only -t uncommitted\n+```\n+\n+**Review against a branch:**\n+\n+```bash\n+cr review --prompt-only --base main\n+```\n+\n+**Review a specific commit range:**\n+\n+```bash\n+cr review --prompt-only --base-commit abc123\n+```","path":".openhands/skills/code-review/SKILL.md","commit_id":"f368f27232e9cc3943237cc84bbef915fe161944","original_commit_id":"9b60f956f9195b3e505aa3f916cb73e383f89da1","user":{"login":"coderabbitai[bot]","id":136622811,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCCSy2w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/347564?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/coderabbitai","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/coderabbitai%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"_⚠️ `backgroundColor`, `font-weight` → `fontWeight` [1][2].  \n- **Single-word properties stay lowercase:** `color`, `margin`, etc. [1]  \n- **Special case:** CSS `float` becomes **`cssFloat`** (since `float` is a JS keyword) [2].  \n- **Vendor prefixes are also camelCased and correctly capitalized:** e.g. `WebkitTransition` (capital “W”), and `msTransition` (lowercase “m”) [3].\n\n**Examples**\n```jsx\n<div style={{ backgroundColor: \"red\", fontWeight: 600, cssFloat: \"left\" }} />\n<div style={{ WebkitTransition: \"all 200ms\", msTransition: \"all 200ms\" }} />\n```\n\nSources: [1] React docs (Common components) ([react.dev](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/common?utm_source=openai)), [2] MDN (CSSStyleProperties / CSSOM naming) IEngineSourceConfig>` のキャストも `IEngineConfig[\"sources\"]` の構造的型情報（`main` の必須性）を消去しており、型安全の観点から二重に問題があります。\n\n<details>\n<summary>🐛 修正案</summary>\n\n```diff\n-  const registrySources =\n-    OfficialRegistry.resolve(\"stockfish\", config.version) || {};\n-  const mergedConfig: IEngineConfig = {\n-    ...config,\n-    sources: {\n-      ...(registrySources as Record<string, IEngineSourceConfig>),\n-      ...(config.sources || {}),\n-    } as Required<IEngineConfig>[\"sources\"],\n-  };\n+  const registrySources: IEngineConfig[\"sources\"] | undefined =\n+    OfficialRegistry.resolve(\"stockfish\", config.version);\n+\n+  // registrySources も config.sources も未指定の場合は sources を設定しない\n+  // (StockfishAdapter 内部の deepMerge がデフォルトを補完する)\n+  const mergedSources: IEngineConfig[\"sources\"] | undefined =\n+    registrySources || config.sources\n+      ? {\n+          ...(registrySources ?? {}),\n+          ...(config.sources ?? {}),\n+        } as Required<IEngineConfig>[\"sources\"]\n+      : undefined;\n+\n+  const mergedConfig: IEngineConfig = {\n+    ...config,\n+    ...(mergedSources ? { sources: mergedSources } : {}),\n+  };\n```\n\nまた、`OfficialRegistry.resolve()` の戻り値型を `IEngineConfig[\"sources\"] | undefined` に整合させることで、キャスト自体を不要にすることが理想的です。\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI sources object that contains a main entry (e.g. prefer\nregistrySources.main, then config.sources.main, otherwise throw or provide a\nclear default), and remove both \"as Record<string, Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles without manual intervention.\n+\n+## Capabilities\n+\n+- Finds bugs, security issues, and quality risks in changed code\n+- Groups findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Info)\n+- Works on staged, committed, or all changes; supports base branch/commit\n+- Provides fix suggestions (`--plain`) or minimal output for agents (`--prompt-only`)\n+\n+## When to Use\n+\n+When user asks to:\n+\n+- Review code changes / Review my code\n+- Check code quality / Find bugs or security issues\n+- Get PR feedback / Pull request review\n+- What's wrong with my code / my changes\n+- Run coderabbit / Use coderabbit\n+\n+## How to Review\n+\n+### 1. Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles without manual intervention.\n+\n+## Capabilities\n+\n+- Finds bugs, security issues, and quality risks in changed code\n+- Groups findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Info)\n+- Works on staged, committed, or all changes; supports base branch/commit\n+- Provides fix suggestions (`--plain`) or minimal output for agents (`--prompt-only`)\n+\n+## When to Use\n+\n+When user asks to:\n+\n+- Review code changes / Review my code\n+- Check code quality / Find bugs or security issues\n+- Get PR feedback / Pull request review\n+- What's wrong with my code / my changes\n+- Run coderabbit / Use coderabbit\n+\n+## How to Review\n+\n+### 1.
